article_title: Principles relating to processing of personal data
summary: The six core principles: lawfulness/fairness/transparency, purpose limitation, data minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity and confidentiality. Plus accountability — controller must be able to demonstrate compliance.
implementation_guidance: Establish a Data Processing Inventory (record of processing activities under Art-30). Map every processing activity to a lawful basis. Define purpose for each dataset. Set retention schedule per data category. Document accuracy mechanisms. Implement security controls per Art-32. Maintain accountability evidence (policies, training records, DPIAs).
evidence_artifacts: Privacy policy, processing inventory, lawful basis register, retention schedule, accountability framework document.

summary: Six lawful bases for processing: consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, legitimate interests.
implementation_guidance: For every processing activity, identify and document one of the six lawful bases. For 'legitimate interests', conduct and document a Legitimate Interests Assessment (LIA). For 'consent', ensure mechanisms meet Art-7. Update lawful basis register when processing purposes change.
evidence_artifacts: Lawful basis register, LIA documents, consent records.

summary: Consent must be freely given, specific, informed, unambiguous, demonstrable, and easily withdrawable.
implementation_guidance: Implement granular consent mechanisms (separate consent per purpose). Pre-ticked boxes are not valid. Maintain consent records (who, what, when, how, version of notice). Provide withdrawal mechanism as easy as giving consent. Document language used in consent requests.
evidence_artifacts: Consent management platform logs, consent UI screenshots, withdrawal mechanism documentation.

article_title: Conditions applicable to child's consent (information society services)
summary: Children below 16 (or lower per Member State, minimum 13) require parental consent for online services.
implementation_guidance: Implement age verification. Define parental consent mechanism. Consider Member State variations in age threshold. Provide age-appropriate notices. Block service or downgrade to processing-free mode for unverified minors.
evidence_artifacts: Age-gate implementation records, parental consent logs, age-appropriate notice copies.

article_title: Processing of special categories of personal data
summary: Special categories (race, ethnicity, political views, religion, union membership, genetics, biometrics, health, sex life, sexual orientation) require explicit consent or one of nine specific exceptions.
implementation_guidance: Identify special category data in inventory. Document specific Art-9(2) exception relied upon. For biometrics used for identification, apply explicit consent or specific legal basis. Apply enhanced security controls (encryption, restricted access). DPIA usually required.
evidence_artifacts: Special category register, explicit consent records, DPIAs.

article_title: Processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions
summary: Criminal-conviction data only processed under official authority or specific Member State law.
implementation_guidance: Verify the authority or legal basis under Member State law before processing. Maintain register of any criminal-record processing. Apply restricted-access controls.
evidence_artifacts: Authority documentation, register of criminal-record processing.

article_title: Transparent information and modalities for exercise of rights
summary: Information must be concise, transparent, intelligible, easily accessible, in plain language, especially for children. Free of charge except for manifestly unfounded/excessive requests.
implementation_guidance: Layered privacy notice (short notice + full notice). Use plain language; avoid legalese. Test readability with target audiences. Define DSAR intake channels (email, web form, phone). Set 1-month response deadline (extendable by 2 months for complex cases). Document any refusals with reason.
evidence_artifacts: Privacy notice text, DSAR intake procedures, response logs.

article_title: Information to be provided where data collected from data subject
summary: At point of collection, must provide identity/contact of controller and DPO, purposes, lawful basis, recipients, transfers, retention period, rights, complaints info.
implementation_guidance: Maintain master privacy notice. Reference it from every collection point (web form, paper form, phone script). Update when purposes change. Translate for non-native-language users. Provide layered presentation.
evidence_artifacts: Privacy notice (current and prior versions), collection-point linkage records.

article_title: Information to be provided where data not obtained from data subject
summary: When data is obtained from third parties, controller must inform the data subject within 1 month, or at first communication, with similar info as Art-13 plus categories of data and source.
implementation_guidance: Establish process to identify data acquired from third parties. Build notification mechanism. Track 1-month deadline. Document Art-14(5) exemptions (impossibility, disproportionate effort, legal obligation, professional secrecy) where applied.
evidence_artifacts: Third-party acquisition log, notification records, exemption documentation.

article_title: Right of access by the data subject
summary: Data subject can request confirmation of processing and a copy of the data, plus information about purposes, categories, recipients, retention, rights, source, and automated decision-making.
implementation_guidance: Implement DSAR process. Verify identity of requester. Compile data from all systems within 1 month. Redact third-party data unless they consented to disclosure. Provide in commonly used electronic format. Document any reductions or refusals.
evidence_artifacts: DSAR procedure, response template, response log with timestamps.

summary: Data subject can request correction of inaccurate or completion of incomplete data.
implementation_guidance: Build correction workflow. Cascade corrections to backups, archives, and recipients per Art-19. Track requests with response timestamps.
evidence_artifacts: Rectification log, recipient-notification records.

article_title: Right to erasure (right to be forgotten)
summary: Data subject can request deletion in specific circumstances (no longer needed, withdrawal of consent, objection, unlawful processing, legal obligation, child's data).
implementation_guidance: Build erasure workflow covering primary systems, backups (with documented retention period before purge), and recipient notifications per Art-19. Document Art-17(3) exemptions where applied (free expression, legal obligations, archiving). Pseudonymization may not satisfy erasure for sensitive cases.
evidence_artifacts: Erasure procedure, deletion log including backup-purge timestamps.

summary: Data subject can demand temporary suspension of processing (e.g., during accuracy disputes or pending legitimate-interests assessment).
implementation_guidance: Implement 'restrict' flag in processing systems. Block all processing except storage. Notify data subject before lifting restriction. Cascade restriction to recipients.
evidence_artifacts: Restriction flag mechanism, restriction log.

article_title: Notification obligation regarding rectification, erasure, restriction
summary: Controller must notify each recipient of data when rectification, erasure, or restriction occurs, unless impossible or disproportionate.
implementation_guidance: Maintain recipient registry per processing activity. Automate cascade notifications where possible. Document where notification was deemed impossible/disproportionate.

summary: Where processing is by automated means based on consent or contract, data subject can receive their data in a structured, machine-readable format and transmit to another controller.
implementation_guidance: Identify processing activities meeting Art-20 criteria. Build export functionality (JSON, XML, CSV). Verify identity. Note that Art-20 does not apply to processing under legitimate interests or legal obligation.
evidence_artifacts: Export format specification, export procedure, export log.

summary: Data subject can object to processing under legitimate interests, public task, or for direct marketing. Direct marketing objection is absolute.
implementation_guidance: Build objection mechanism. For legitimate-interests objections, conduct override assessment (compelling legitimate grounds). For direct marketing, stop immediately and unconditionally. Maintain marketing suppression list.

summary: Data subject has right not to be subject to solely automated decisions producing legal/significant effects, with limited exceptions (contract, EU/Member State law, explicit consent).
implementation_guidance: Identify automated decision-making in scope. Provide human-review mechanism. Inform data subject before decision is made. For ML/AI systems making consequential decisions, document the logic, build explanation capability, and align with ISO 42001 controls. DPIA likely required.
evidence_artifacts: Automated decision register, human-review process, model documentation, DPIA.

summary: Controller must implement technical and organizational measures to ensure and demonstrate compliance, considering nature/scope/context/purposes of processing and risks.
implementation_guidance: Establish privacy management system. Adopt approved codes of conduct or certifications where available. Maintain accountability evidence (policies, training records, audits, DPIAs).

article_title: Data protection by design and by default
summary: Privacy must be built into system design and default configuration must be the most privacy-protective.
implementation_guidance: Integrate privacy review gates into SDLC. Default to minimum data, shortest retention, least sharing, strongest access controls. Document privacy-design decisions. Conduct DPIAs at design stage.
evidence_artifacts: Privacy-by-design checklist, design-review records.

summary: Where two or more controllers jointly determine purposes/means, they must determine respective responsibilities by arrangement and make essence available to data subjects.
implementation_guidance: Identify joint-controller relationships. Sign joint-controller agreements (Art-26 agreements) covering responsibilities, contact point, transparency obligations. Publish summary.

article_title: Representatives of controllers/processors not established in the EU
summary: Non-EU controllers/processors offering goods/services to EU subjects or monitoring EU subjects must designate an EU representative.
implementation_guidance: Determine if Art-3(2) applies. Designate written representative in EU Member State where data subjects are. Maintain authorisation. Inform data subjects of representative.

summary: Controllers must use only processors providing sufficient guarantees. Data Processing Agreement (DPA) must cover specific subjects (subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, types of data, categories of subjects, rights/obligations of controller).
implementation_guidance: Vendor due-diligence process. DPA template aligned with Art-28(3). Sub-processor authorization process. Audit rights documented and exercised. Maintain register of all processors with DPA status. Annual review.
evidence_artifacts: Vendor questionnaire, signed DPAs, sub-processor registers, vendor audit reports.

summary: Controllers and processors (>250 employees, or with high-risk/special-category processing) must maintain records of processing activities (RoPA) including specific information.
implementation_guidance: Implement RoPA system. Capture all required Art-30(1) fields for controllers, Art-30(2) for processors. Annual review. Make available to supervisory authority on request.
evidence_artifacts: Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) register.

summary: Implement appropriate technical and organisational measures: pseudonymisation/encryption, ongoing CIA, restoration after incidents, regular testing.
implementation_guidance: Map to ISO 27001 / NCA ECC controls. Encrypt data at rest and in transit. Implement access control, logging, monitoring. Regular security testing (penetration tests, vulnerability scans). Backup and DR. Incident response procedures. Personnel training. Document risk-based justification for chosen measures.
evidence_artifacts: Security policy, encryption configurations, access logs, pen-test reports, training records.
iso27701_xref: Maps to ISO 27001 Annex A; ISO 27701 §6 controls

article_title: Notification of personal data breach to supervisory authority
summary: Controller must notify SA within 72 hours of becoming aware of a breach (unless unlikely to result in risk). Processor must notify controller without undue delay.
implementation_guidance: Incident response plan with privacy-specific track. 72h timer mechanism. Breach assessment template. Contact info for relevant SAs. Documented criteria for 'unlikely to result in risk'. Mock breach drills. Maintain breach register including those not notified.
evidence_artifacts: Breach response plan, breach register, breach drill records, SA notification correspondence.

article_title: Communication of personal data breach to data subject
summary: Where breach likely to result in high risk to data subjects, communicate to them without undue delay. Exceptions: encryption rendering data unintelligible; subsequent measures eliminating high risk; disproportionate effort.
implementation_guidance: Risk-tiering model for breach severity. Communication templates in plain language. Channel selection (email, post, public announcement if disproportionate). Document any reliance on exceptions.

summary: DPIA required where processing likely to result in high risk: systematic profiling, large-scale special-category, large-scale public monitoring, others per SA list.
implementation_guidance: DPIA trigger criteria documented. Standard DPIA template covering Art-35(7) elements (description, necessity/proportionality, risk assessment, mitigations). DPO involvement. Stakeholder consultation. Sign-off process. Annual review.
evidence_artifacts: DPIA template, completed DPIAs, DPIA register.

article_title: Prior consultation with the supervisory authority
summary: Where DPIA indicates high residual risk after mitigations, controller must consult SA before processing.
implementation_guidance: Define residual-risk threshold triggering consultation. Standard consultation submission package. Track SA response timeline (8 weeks +6 weeks extension).

article_title: Designation of the data protection officer (DPO)
summary: DPO mandatory for: public authorities, large-scale systematic monitoring, or large-scale special-category/criminal-data processing.
implementation_guidance: Determine DPO requirement. Appoint qualified DPO (expert in data protection law and practices). DPO contact info published and notified to SA. DPO independence and resourcing protected. DPO position can be internal or external/group-shared.
evidence_artifacts: DPO appointment letter, DPO contact info publication, SA notification.

summary: DPO must be involved in all data-protection issues, given resources, report to highest management, no instructions on tasks, no dismissal/penalty for performing tasks.
implementation_guidance: DPO included in all relevant meetings. Direct reporting line to top management. Adequate budget and tools. Cannot be punished for compliance findings. Document DPO independence.

summary: Inform/advise organization, monitor compliance, provide DPIA advice, cooperate with SA, act as SA contact.
implementation_guidance: DPO RACI documented. DPO advice records on each DPIA. DPO presence in compliance reviews. DPO-led training. DPO-SA correspondence registry.

summary: Transfer outside EU/EEA only if conditions in Art-45-49 met.
implementation_guidance: Maintain transfer mapping per processing activity. Identify safeguard mechanism per transfer (adequacy, SCC, BCR, exception). Annual review.

article_title: Transfers on the basis of an adequacy decision
summary: Transfers permitted to countries with EU adequacy decision (e.g., UK, Switzerland, Canada-private-sector).
implementation_guidance: Track current adequacy decisions list (changes periodically). Document basis in transfer register.

summary: Where no adequacy, use SCCs (Standard Contractual Clauses), BCRs (Binding Corporate Rules), approved codes of conduct, or approved certification.
implementation_guidance: Adopt 2021 EU SCCs (Module 1-4 selection). Conduct Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) per Schrems II. Implement supplementary measures (encryption, contractual rights, enhanced security). Document TIA outcomes.
evidence_artifacts: Signed SCCs, TIA documents, supplementary-measure descriptions.

summary: Limited exceptions: explicit consent, contract necessity, important reasons of public interest, legal claims, vital interests, public-register data.
implementation_guidance: Use only where Art-45-46 unavailable. Document the specific Art-49 ground. Note these are narrow exceptions, not routine bases.

article_title: Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
summary: Data subjects can complain to SA in their habitual residence, place of work, or alleged infringement.
implementation_guidance: Inform data subjects of right in privacy notices. Maintain SA-correspondence registry.

summary: Anyone suffering material/non-material damage from infringement has right to compensation.
implementation_guidance: Insurance review (cyber/privacy insurance). Indemnification clauses in DPAs. Litigation tracking process.

article_title: General conditions for imposing administrative fines
summary: Tiered fines: up to €10M or 2% global turnover, or €20M or 4% global turnover for serious violations.
implementation_guidance: Risk-tier processing activities by potential fine exposure. Board-level reporting on residual fine exposure. Mitigation tracking. Document compliance evidence to demonstrate good faith if SA investigates.

summary: Specific fields a controller's RoPA must capture.
implementation_guidance: Per Art-30(1): controller name+contact, joint controller info, DPO contact, processing purposes, categories of data subjects, categories of personal data, categories of recipients including third countries, transfers, time limits for erasure, technical/organisational security measures.

summary: Specific fields a processor's RoPA must capture.
implementation_guidance: Per Art-30(2): processor name+contact, controller name+contact, controller's DPO contact, categories of processing carried out for each controller, transfers to third countries, technical/organisational security measures.

summary: Information should be in clear, plain language; identifying purposes must be specific, explicit and legitimate.
implementation_guidance: Style guide for privacy notices. Test readability with target audience. Multilingual support where needed.

summary: Further processing for new purpose only where compatible; consider link to original purpose, context, nature of data, consequences, safeguards.
implementation_guidance: Compatibility-assessment template applied before any new use of existing data.

summary: Profiling carries specific risks; subjects should have right to obtain human intervention, express their view, and contest decisions.
implementation_guidance: Map to Art-22 requirements; include in DPIA where applicable.
